mission.title: istio: Istio sidecar injection failing with error - MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume "istiod-ca-cert" : configmap "istio-ca-root-cert" not found
mission.description: Istio sidecar injection failing with error - MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume "istiod-ca-cert" : configmap "istio-ca-root-cert" not found. This issue affects 37+ users.

steps.title: Identify istio troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your istio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=istio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Istio sidecar injection failing with error - MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume "istiod-ca-cert" : configmap "istio-ca-root-cert" not found

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Bug Description**
Istiod is not responding to new namespaces. Created a new namespace and labelled istio-injectio=enabled. I deployed one app to the namespace and the pod is stuck in init stage. Ist

```yaml
# istioctl version
client version: 1.5.0
control plane version: 1.5.0
data plane version: 1.5.0 (17 proxies)
```

steps.title: Confirm Istio sidecar injection failing with error - Mount is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Might fix https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/22463, was found during
debugging this.

https://github.com/istio/istio/pull/21669 made a change that set the
stop channel to the global stop channel instead of leader election stop
channel, so it doesn't stop when leader is lost. Once that is fixed we
need to create new informers, or client-go will crash due to closing the
same informer twice. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
# istioctl version
client version: 1.5.0
control plane version: 1.5.0
data plane version: 1.5.0 (17 proxies)

# kubectl create namespace keycloak
namespace/keycloak created
# kubectl label namespace keycloak istio-injection=enabled
namespace/keycloak labeled
# kubectl describe namespaces keycloak
Name:         keycloak
Labels:       istio-injection=enabled
Annotations:  <none>
Status:       Active

No resource quota.

No LimitRange resource.
# kubectl get configmaps -n keycloak
No resources found in keycloak namespace.
# kubectl get configmaps -n default
NAME                 DATA   AGE
istio-ca-root-cert   1      10d

istioctl manifest apply \
     --set values.grafana.enabled=true \
     --set values.prometheus.enabled=true \
     --set values.kiali.enabled=true --set values.kiali.createDemoSecret=true \
     --set values.global.proxy.accessLogFile="/dev/dtdout"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
